title: leonurus japonicus f. albiflorus
intro: Leonurus japonicus f. albiflorus, also known as White-Flowered Lion's Tail, is a plant species within the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its white flowers and distinctive, lion's tail-like inflorescence. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating menstrual disorders, menopausal symptoms, and anxiety, due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Leonurus japonicus f. albiflorus include reducing stress, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of premenstrual syndrome. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying the flowers and leaves, which can be made into teas, infusions, or capsules for consumption.
